text: reminded me of sitting in the back seat on long drives, she'd always have those wrapped caramels in her purse
the exact smell of being hugged goodnight as a kid
my dad's wallet smelled like this, i haven't thought about that in years
first day of school every year, new pencils and a clean eraser
found my grandma's suitcase in the attic, it still smelled like her house
i used to keep letters and photos in a shoebox under my childhood bed
winter mornings before school, standing next to the radiator to warm up

scent: lamp that has been on all night
text: staying up too late reading, the lamp getting warm, everyone else asleep
late nights finishing homework, the laptop fan going full speed
visiting someone in the hospital, the elevator smell mixed with cleaning chemicals
moving to a new city, the first time i took the subway alone
sneaking into a second movie as a teenager, the empty theater between screenings
digging in the garden with my mom after a storm, everything smelled clean
summer rain on the basketball court outside our apartment building
walking home from school in october, kicking wet leaves on the sidewalk
birthday candles, the smoke after everyone sings, the wish already made
the plastic smell of a halloween mask, running door to door in the dark
counting change as a kid to see if we had enough for the corner store
opening a brand new box of crayons on the first day of school, picking which color to use first
walking into school too early, the halls empty and echoing, everything smelling like cleaning supplies
hiding under the slide during tag, the plastic warm from the sun and dirt sticking to my hands
being wrapped up after a shower, the blanket still warm from the dryer
pulling my hands into my sleeves during class, feeling safe and hidden
waking up disoriented in the afternoon, the pillow still warm and slightly damp
the sharp plastic smell filling the bathroom when we first hung it up

scent: opening a new inflatable pool toy
text: blowing up a pool float in the backyard, the plastic smell mixing with summer air
back to school shopping, opening binders and smelling the plastic and paper
getting into the car after it sat in the sun all day, the seat almost burning
leaving the pool and smelling chlorine on my skin hours later
someone rubbing sunscreen into my shoulders at the beach before going into the water
cool air coming through the window, hearing distant sounds from outside

scent: TV left on in a dark room
text: falling asleep with the TV still on, waking up to it glowing in the dark
being awake in the middle of the night, everything feeling still and unreal
the smell of toast in the morning before school, everything quiet
standing at the sink late at night, hands in warm water, everything slowed down
opening the microwave and smelling whatever was just heated lingering inside
being alone in the garage, the smell of oil and concrete making it feel closed in
opening the mailbox and feeling the heat from inside, hoping something was there
drinking from the hose as a kid, the water tasting metallic and warm at first
hugging someone goodbye and their scent staying on your clothes after
standing close enough to notice the warmth and scent of someone's skin
holding hands and becoming aware of it, not knowing when to let go
holding my phone too long while waiting for a message, it getting warm
losing track of time while playing, hands slightly sweaty on the controller
opening a new device and smelling that clean, new electronic smell
